title: Dense Crispy Uruguayan Turkey Neck Stew
description: A winey, smoky stew with crispy turkey necks and hearty vegetables, perfect for adventurous diners.
instructions: Heat olive oil in large kazan cauldron over medium-high until shimmering.
Add smoked turkey necks and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove necks and set aside; pour wine into cauldron and whisk until fully incorporated.
Add smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, bay leaf, salt, pepper and stir for 30 seconds.
Return necks to cauldron, pour in chicken stock, bring to a gentle simmer.
Add diced potatoes, carrots and kale, cover and cook for 20 minutes until vegetables tender.
Reduce heat to low, whisk in balsamic vinegar and adjust seasoning; maintain a slow simmer for 10 more minutes.
Serve hot, letting the crispy necks and dense broth mingle; garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
